subject: UX & Research
title: The Digg v4 Meltdown
description: A quick win: understand The Digg v4 Meltdown.

hook.text: Digg was the king of social news. Then version 4 launched in 2010, and the community fled to Reddit.

buildup.text: Digg v4 removed the democratic voting system. Power shifted to publishers and promoted content over users.

discovery.text: Users revolted. The front page was flooded with links to Reddit as a protest against the new design.

twist.text: Traffic dropped 26% in a single month. Digg never recovered and was eventually sold for just $500,000.

climax.text: The redesign didn't just change pixels. It changed who had power. Users felt demoted and left.

punchline.text: A redesign that takes power from users takes users too.

quiz.question: Why did Digg v4 fail so catastrophically?
options: It removed the democratic voting system and shifted power to publishers
The new design was visually unappealing
The site became too slow to load
